pages.text: <name> had always loved inventing. One day, he decided to build a robot that could roll rotis automatically. He imagined a neat kitchen, warm flatbreads popping out perfectly every few seconds, and him lounging with a glass of mango lassi.
After days of tinkering, soldering, and testing, the robot — which he named RotiBot — was ready. <name> placed a ball of dough on its tiny conveyor belt and pressed the shiny red button.
The machine whirred to life, gears spinning, arms waving, and… whizz! The first roti shot out like a frisbee and landed on the neighbor’s roof. 'Uh-oh,' muttered <name>.
The next roti smashed into the garden gnome, bouncing off like a rubber ball. The cat, seeing flying food, pounced enthusiastically and slid across the kitchen floor in a flurry of dough.
RotiBot started rolling rotis faster and faster. Soon, the living room looked like a mini roti storm. Rotis flew onto the sofa, bounced off walls, and one even landed perfectly on the dog’s head!

The dog shook it off, barking excitedly, which only made the robot speed up more. <name> grabbed a broom and tried to chase RotiBot, but it zipped under tables, behind chairs, and through the hallway.
'Stop! Stop!' he shouted, slipping on a stray roti. He tumbled into a pile of pillows, laughing despite the chaos. The kitchen was now covered in dough, flying rotis, and giggles.
Finally, <name> remembered the robot’s emergency switch — hidden under a flap he had forgotten to label. He dove, pressed it, and RotiBot whirred down to a stop with a gentle 'beep-beep.'

Neighbors peeked in, holding their stomachs. 'Did your robot just attack us with rotis?' one asked, giggling. <name> held up the perfectly rolled rotis still stacked on the counter. 'Well… it works! Just a little… enthusiastically.'

Soon, everyone — neighbors, pets, and family — gathered in the garden. They laughed as they shared rotis, dipping them in butter and chutney. <name> realized that inventions could go wild, but sometimes the best part was sharing the fun — and the food — with everyone.
